testcase.name: AtlaspackV3 builds
javascript should produce a basic JS bundle with CommonJS requires
testcase.classname: should produce a basic JS bundle with CommonJS requires

testcase.name: javascript should produce a basic JS bundle with ES6 imports
testcase.classname: should produce a basic JS bundle with ES6 imports

testcase.name: javascript should detect dependencies inserted by a prior transform
testcase.classname: should detect dependencies inserted by a prior transform

testcase.name: javascript should ignore unused requires after process.env inlining
testcase.classname: should ignore unused requires after process.env inlining

testcase.name: javascript should produce a basic JS bundle with object rest spread support
testcase.classname: should produce a basic JS bundle with object rest spread support

testcase.name: javascript should bundle node_modules for a browser environment
testcase.classname: should bundle node_modules for a browser environment

testcase.name: javascript should not bundle node_modules for a node environment
testcase.classname: should not bundle node_modules for a node environment

testcase.name: javascript should preserve hashbangs in bundles and preserve executable file mode
testcase.classname: should preserve hashbangs in bundles and preserve executable file mode

testcase.name: javascript should not preserve hashbangs in browser bundles
testcase.classname: should not preserve hashbangs in browser bundles

testcase.name: javascript should preserve hashbangs in scopehoisted bundles
testcase.classname: should preserve hashbangs in scopehoisted bundles

testcase.name: javascript should bundle node_modules for a node environment if includeNodeModules is specified
testcase.classname: should bundle node_modules for a node environment if includeNodeModules is specified

testcase.name: javascript should bundle builtins for a browser environment
testcase.classname: should bundle builtins for a browser environment

testcase.name: javascript should not bundle builtins for a node environment if includeNodeModules is specified
testcase.classname: should not bundle builtins for a node environment if includeNodeModules is specified

testcase.name: javascript should produce a JS bundle with default exports and no imports
testcase.classname: should produce a JS bundle with default exports and no imports

testcase.name: javascript should split bundles when a dynamic import is used a browser environment
testcase.classname: should split bundles when a dynamic import is used a browser environment

testcase.name: javascript should split bundles when a dynamic import is used with a node environment
testcase.classname: should split bundles when a dynamic import is used with a node environment

testcase.name: javascript should split bundles when a dynamic import is used with an electron-main environment
testcase.classname: should split bundles when a dynamic import is used with an electron-main environment

testcase.name: javascript should split bundles when a dynamic import is used with an electron-renderer environment
testcase.classname: should split bundles when a dynamic import is used with an electron-renderer environment

testcase.name: javascript dynamic imports loaded as high-priority scripts when not all engines support esmodules natively
testcase.classname: dynamic imports loaded as high-priority scripts when not all engines support esmodules natively

testcase.name: javascript should deduplicate and remove an unnecessary async bundle when it contains a cyclic reference to its entry
testcase.classname: should deduplicate and remove an unnecessary async bundle when it contains a cyclic reference to its entry

testcase.name: javascript does not create bundles for dynamic imports when assets are available up the graph
testcase.classname: does not create bundles for dynamic imports when assets are available up the graph

testcase.name: javascript should return all exports as an object when using ES modules
testcase.classname: should return all exports as an object when using ES modules

testcase.name: javascript should duplicate small modules across multiple bundles
testcase.classname: should duplicate small modules across multiple bundles

testcase.name: javascript should create a separate bundle for large modules shared between bundles
testcase.classname: should create a separate bundle for large modules shared between bundles

testcase.name: javascript should not duplicate a module which is already in a parent bundle
testcase.classname: should not duplicate a module which is already in a parent bundle

testcase.name: javascript should duplicate an asset if it is not present in every parent bundle
testcase.classname: should duplicate an asset if it is not present in every parent bundle

testcase.name: javascript should duplicate an asset if it is not available in all possible ancestries
testcase.classname: should duplicate an asset if it is not available in all possible ancestries

testcase.name: javascript should support shared modules with async imports
testcase.classname: should support shared modules with async imports

testcase.name: javascript should ignore new URL and import.meta.url with local binding
testcase.classname: should ignore new URL and import.meta.url with local binding

testcase.name: javascript should minify JS in production mode
testcase.classname: should minify JS in production mode

testcase.name: javascript should use terser config
testcase.classname: should use terser config

testcase.name: javascript should insert global variables when needed
testcase.classname: should insert global variables when needed

testcase.name: javascript should replace __dirname and __filename with path relative to asset.filePath
testcase.classname: should replace __dirname and __filename with path relative to asset.filePath

testcase.name: javascript should replace __dirname and __filename with path relative to asset.filePath with scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should replace __dirname and __filename with path relative to asset.filePath with scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should work when multiple files use globals with scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should work when multiple files use globals with scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should not insert global variables when used in a module specifier
testcase.classname: should not insert global variables when used in a module specifier

testcase.name: javascript should not insert global variables in dead branches
testcase.classname: should not insert global variables in dead branches

testcase.name: javascript should handle re-declaration of the global constant
testcase.classname: should handle re-declaration of the global constant

testcase.name: javascript should not insert environment variables in node environment
testcase.classname: should not insert environment variables in node environment

testcase.name: javascript should not replace process.env.hasOwnProperty with undefined
testcase.classname: should not replace process.env.hasOwnProperty with undefined

testcase.name: javascript should insert the user's NODE_ENV as process.env.NODE_ENV if passed
testcase.classname: should insert the user's NODE_ENV as process.env.NODE_ENV if passed

testcase.name: javascript should not inline computed accesses to process.env
testcase.classname: should not inline computed accesses to process.env

testcase.name: javascript should inline computed accesses with string literals to process.env
testcase.classname: should inline computed accesses with string literals to process.env

testcase.name: javascript should replace process.browser for target browser
testcase.classname: should replace process.browser for target browser

testcase.name: javascript should resolve the browser field before main
testcase.classname: should resolve the browser field before main

testcase.name: javascript should resolve the main field
testcase.classname: should resolve the main field

testcase.name: javascript should ignore require if it is defined in the scope
testcase.classname: should ignore require if it is defined in the scope

testcase.name: javascript should expose to CommonJS entry point
testcase.classname: should expose to CommonJS entry point

testcase.name: javascript should expose to RequireJS entry point
testcase.classname: should expose to RequireJS entry point

testcase.name: javascript should package successfully with comments on last line
testcase.classname: should package successfully with comments on last line

testcase.name: javascript should package successfully with comments on last line and minification
testcase.classname: should package successfully with comments on last line and minification

testcase.name: javascript should package successfully with comments on last line and scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should package successfully with comments on last line and scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should package successfully with comments on last line, scope hoisting and minification
testcase.classname: should package successfully with comments on last line, scope hoisting and minification

testcase.name: javascript should not replace toplevel this with undefined in CommonJS without scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: should not replace toplevel this with undefined in CommonJS without scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should not replace toplevel this with undefined in CommonJS when scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: should not replace toplevel this with undefined in CommonJS when scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should replace toplevel this with undefined in ESM without scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: should replace toplevel this with undefined in ESM without scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should replace toplevel this with undefined in ESM when scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: should replace toplevel this with undefined in ESM when scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should support async importing the same module from different bundles
testcase.classname: should support async importing the same module from different bundles

testcase.name: javascript should not create shared bundles from contents of entries
testcase.classname: should not create shared bundles from contents of entries

testcase.name: javascript should import the same dependency multiple times in the same bundle
testcase.classname: should import the same dependency multiple times in the same bundle

testcase.name: javascript should detect typescript style async requires in commonjs
testcase.classname: should detect typescript style async requires in commonjs

testcase.name: javascript should detect typescript style async requires in commonjs with esModuleInterop flag
testcase.classname: should detect typescript style async requires in commonjs with esModuleInterop flag

testcase.name: javascript should detect typescript style async requires in commonjs with esModuleInterop flag and arrow functions
testcase.classname: should detect typescript style async requires in commonjs with esModuleInterop flag and arrow functions

testcase.name: javascript should detect rollup style async requires in commonjs
testcase.classname: should detect rollup style async requires in commonjs

testcase.name: javascript should only detect requires that are returned from the promise
testcase.classname: should only detect requires that are returned from the promise

testcase.name: javascript should properly chain a dynamic import wrapped in a Promise.resolve()
testcase.classname: should properly chain a dynamic import wrapped in a Promise.resolve()

testcase.name: javascript should detect parcel style async requires in commonjs
testcase.classname: should detect parcel style async requires in commonjs

testcase.name: javascript can static import and dynamic import in the same bundle without creating a new bundle
testcase.classname: can static import and dynamic import in the same bundle without creating a new bundle

testcase.name: javascript async dependency can be resolved internally and externally from two different bundles
testcase.classname: async dependency can be resolved internally and externally from two different bundles

testcase.name: javascript can static import and dynamic import in the same bundle ancestry without creating a new bundle
testcase.classname: can static import and dynamic import in the same bundle ancestry without creating a new bundle

testcase.name: javascript can static import and dynamic import in the same bundle when another bundle requires async
testcase.classname: can static import and dynamic import in the same bundle when another bundle requires async

testcase.name: javascript can share dependencies between a shared bundle and its sibling's descendants
testcase.classname: can share dependencies between a shared bundle and its sibling's descendants

testcase.name: javascript can run an entry bundle whose entry asset is present in another bundle
testcase.classname: can run an entry bundle whose entry asset is present in another bundle

testcase.name: javascript can run an async bundle whose entry asset is present in another bundle
testcase.classname: can run an async bundle whose entry asset is present in another bundle

testcase.name: javascript can create a bundle starting with a dot
testcase.classname: can create a bundle starting with a dot

testcase.name: javascript should not automatically name bundle files starting with a dot
testcase.classname: should not automatically name bundle files starting with a dot

testcase.name: javascript should support duplicate re-exports without scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should support duplicate re-exports without scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should prioritize named exports before re-exports withput scope hoisting (before)
testcase.classname: should prioritize named exports before re-exports withput scope hoisting (before)

testcase.name: javascript should prioritize named exports before re-exports without scope hoisting (after)
testcase.classname: should prioritize named exports before re-exports without scope hoisting (after)

testcase.name: javascript should exclude default from export all declaration
testcase.classname: should exclude default from export all declaration

testcase.name: javascript should support import namespace declarations of other ES modules
testcase.classname: should support import namespace declarations of other ES modules

testcase.name: javascript should support import namespace declarations of class from CJS
testcase.classname: should support import namespace declarations of class from CJS

testcase.name: javascript should support import namespace declarations of object from CJS
testcase.classname: should support import namespace declarations of object from CJS

testcase.name: javascript should support export namespace declarations
testcase.classname: should support export namespace declarations

testcase.name: javascript should support export declarations with destructuring
testcase.classname: should support export declarations with destructuring

testcase.name: javascript should support export default declarations
testcase.classname: should support export default declarations

testcase.name: javascript should hoist function default exports to allow circular imports
testcase.classname: should hoist function default exports to allow circular imports

testcase.name: javascript should hoist anonymous function default exports to allow circular imports
testcase.classname: should hoist anonymous function default exports to allow circular imports

testcase.name: javascript should work with many different types of exports
testcase.classname: should work with many different types of exports

testcase.name: javascript should correctly export functions
testcase.classname: should correctly export functions

testcase.name: javascript should handle exports of imports
testcase.classname: should handle exports of imports

testcase.name: javascript should handle simultaneous import and reexports of the same identifier
testcase.classname: should handle simultaneous import and reexports of the same identifier

testcase.name: javascript should generate a unique variable name for imports
testcase.classname: should generate a unique variable name for imports

testcase.name: javascript should not replace identifier with a var declaration inside a for loop
testcase.classname: should not replace identifier with a var declaration inside a for loop

testcase.name: javascript should replace an imported identifier with function locals of the same name
testcase.classname: should replace an imported identifier with function locals of the same name

testcase.name: javascript should replace imported values in member expressions
testcase.classname: should replace imported values in member expressions

testcase.name: javascript should retain the correct dependency order between import and reexports
testcase.classname: should retain the correct dependency order between import and reexports

testcase.name: javascript should not freeze live default imports
testcase.classname: should not freeze live default imports

testcase.name: javascript should not rewrite this in arrow function class properties
testcase.classname: should not rewrite this in arrow function class properties

testcase.name: javascript should call named imports without this context
testcase.classname: should call named imports without this context

testcase.name: javascript should only replace free references to require
testcase.classname: should only replace free references to require

testcase.name: javascript should only replace free references to require with scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should only replace free references to require with scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should support import and non-top-level require of same asset from different assets
testcase.classname: should support import and non-top-level require of same asset from different assets

testcase.name: javascript should support import and non-top-level require of same asset from different assets with scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should support import and non-top-level require of same asset from different assets with scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should support runtime module deduplication
testcase.classname: should support runtime module deduplication

testcase.name: javascript should avoid creating a bundle for lazy dependencies already available in a shared bundle
testcase.classname: should avoid creating a bundle for lazy dependencies already available in a shared bundle

testcase.name: javascript should support standalone import.meta
testcase.classname: should support standalone import.meta

testcase.name: javascript supports ESM imports and requires with sideEffects: false
testcase.classname: supports ESM imports and requires with sideEffects: false

testcase.name: javascript should not affect ESM import order
testcase.classname: should not affect ESM import order

testcase.name: javascript should not affect ESM import order with scope hoisting
testcase.classname: should not affect ESM import order with scope hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should not deduplicate an asset if it will become unreachable
testcase.classname: should not deduplicate an asset if it will become unreachable

testcase.name: javascript should detect shorthand identifier imports
testcase.classname: should detect shorthand identifier imports

testcase.name: javascript should retain unicode escape sequences
testcase.classname: should retain unicode escape sequences

testcase.name: javascript ignores missing unused import specifiers in source assets without scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: ignores missing unused import specifiers in source assets without scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript ignores missing unused import specifiers in node-modules without scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: ignores missing unused import specifiers in node-modules without scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript duplicate assets should share module scope without scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: duplicate assets should share module scope without scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should work correctly with export called hasOwnProperty without scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: should work correctly with export called hasOwnProperty without scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript ignores missing unused import specifiers in source assets with scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: ignores missing unused import specifiers in source assets with scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript ignores missing unused import specifiers in node-modules with scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: ignores missing unused import specifiers in node-modules with scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript duplicate assets should share module scope with scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: duplicate assets should share module scope with scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript should work correctly with export called hasOwnProperty with scope-hoisting
testcase.classname: should work correctly with export called hasOwnProperty with scope-hoisting

testcase.name: javascript multiple import types supports both static and dynamic imports to the same specifier in the same file
testcase.classname: supports both static and dynamic imports to the same specifier in the same file

testcase.name: javascript multiple import types supports static, dynamic, and url to the same specifier in the same file
testcase.classname: supports static, dynamic, and url to the same specifier in the same file

testcase.name: javascript multiple import types supports dynamic import and url to the same specifier in the same file
testcase.classname: supports dynamic import and url to the same specifier in the same file

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports deferring an unused ES6 re-export (wildcard, empty, unused)
testcase.classname: supports deferring an unused ES6 re-export (wildcard, empty, unused)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports chained ES6 re-exports
testcase.classname: supports chained ES6 re-exports

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting should not optimize away an unused ES6 re-export and an used import (different symbols)
testcase.classname: should not optimize away an unused ES6 re-export and an used import (different symbols)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports wildcards
testcase.classname: supports wildcards

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting correctly handles excluded and wrapped reexport assets
testcase.classname: correctly handles excluded and wrapped reexport assets

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports the package.json sideEffects: false flag with shared dependencies and code splitting II
testcase.classname: supports the package.json sideEffects: false flag with shared dependencies and code splitting II

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports namespace imports of theoretically excluded reexporting assets
testcase.classname: supports namespace imports of theoretically excluded reexporting assets

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting can import from a different bundle via a re-export
testcase.classname: can import from a different bundle via a re-export

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports excluding when doing both exports and reexports
testcase.classname: supports excluding when doing both exports and reexports

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports named and renamed reexports of the same asset (named used)
testcase.classname: supports named and renamed reexports of the same asset (named used)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports named and namespace exports of the same asset (namespace used)
testcase.classname: supports named and namespace exports of the same asset (namespace used)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false without scope-hoisting supports deferring non-weak dependencies that are not used
testcase.classname: supports deferring non-weak dependencies that are not used

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports deferring an unused ES6 re-export (wildcard, empty, unused)
testcase.classname: supports deferring an unused ES6 re-export (wildcard, empty, unused)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports chained ES6 re-exports
testcase.classname: supports chained ES6 re-exports

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting should not optimize away an unused ES6 re-export and an used import (different symbols)
testcase.classname: should not optimize away an unused ES6 re-export and an used import (different symbols)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports wildcards
testcase.classname: supports wildcards

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting correctly handles excluded and wrapped reexport assets
testcase.classname: correctly handles excluded and wrapped reexport assets

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports namespace imports of theoretically excluded reexporting assets
testcase.classname: supports namespace imports of theoretically excluded reexporting assets

testcase.classname: can import from a different bundle via a re-export

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports excluding when doing both exports and reexports
testcase.classname: supports excluding when doing both exports and reexports

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports named and renamed reexports of the same asset (named used)
testcase.classname: supports named and renamed reexports of the same asset (named used)

testcase.name: javascript sideEffects: false with scope-hoisting supports named and namespace exports of the same asset (namespace used)
